creator: cyberbotx@cyberbotx.com
text:
Daniel Feenberg wrote:
> However, when I run make I get the same error message as before. Is 
> there a difference between portsnap1.freebsd.org and the server you are 
> consulting? Should I be updating from a different server?

I don't use portsnap, personally, I usually use csup from 
cvsup15.us.freebsd.org.  My devel/mingw32-bin-msvcrt Makefile is dated September 
19, 2009 10:22 (I'm at -4 GMT in the US Eastern time zone).  So I was wrong 
about the "couple weeks" comment, it seems like it'd been more than 5 days for 
some reason.

> In the Makefile I see:
> 
>   APIVERSION     3.13
>   RUNTIMEVERSION 3.16
> 
> Is that right?
> 
> If I ftp tp ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/distfiles/mingw I can see 
> versions of w32api from 3.1 to 3.13, but no 3.16. There are 3.16 
> versions of some other files, but not that one. In any case, the 
> Makefile is looking to find 3.13, and does find it, but then complains 
> the checksum is wrong - perhaps because the checksum is for 3.16? I am 
> out of my depth here.

w32api uses APIVERSION, which is 3.13.  mingwrt is the one which uses 
RUNTIMEVERSION, which is 3.16 now.  I was told that some Sourceforge mirrors 
don't have w32api-3.13-mingw32-dev.tar.gz correctly, but the only one I noticed 
that was wrong was the garr SF mirror, and that one isn't even used by the ports 
system.  What is the MD5 on your copy of w32api-3.13-mingw32-dev.tar.gz?

Thanks,
Naram Qashat
